title: Typing React components
body:
## Definition

Typing React components means annotating props (and optional `children`) — prefer explicit prop interfaces over `React.FC` in modern code.

## Why it exists

Catch missing/extra props at compile time; document component contracts.

## How it works

```tsx
type ButtonProps = { label: string; onClick: () => void };
function Button({ label, onClick }: ButtonProps) { ... }
```

`React.FC` adds implicit `children` — often unwanted.

## Common confusion

`FC` vs plain functions: community prefers plain functions + explicit props for clarity and generic support.

## In practice

Export prop types; use `ComponentProps<"button">` to extend native elements; generics for reusable UI.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

[Typing React components](concept:typescript-react-components) means annotating props (and optional `children`) — prefer explicit prop interfaces over [React.FC](concept:typescript-react-components) in modern code.

## Why it exists

[Catch](concept:try-catch-finally) missing/extra props at compile time; document component contracts.

## How it works

```tsx
type ButtonProps = { label: string; onClick: () => void };
function Button({ label, onClick }: ButtonProps) { ... }
```

[React.FC](concept:typescript-react-components) adds implicit `children` — often unwanted.

## Common confusion

[FC](concept:typescript-react-components) vs plain functions: community prefers plain functions + explicit props for clarity and generic support.

## In practice

Export prop types; use `ComponentProps<"button">` to extend native elements; [generics](concept:typescript-generics) for reusable UI.
